description: Research unknowns for the next plan

content: Please read AGENTS.md and follow its instructions. Then delete `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` if it already exists so this flow cannot inherit stale active-plan state from an earlier run. Do not fail if the file is missing, and do not create a replacement in this step. If you deleted it, commit the change and then, if you can, push.

Now you know the selected plan/spec/story, write or overwrite `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` with only the selected relative plan path using the shape `{ "plan_path": "planning/<story-file>.md" }`. Do not write absolute paths. This handoff file becomes the sole plan-selection source for every later step in this flow. Commit this file, and push if you can

Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Do NOT use code_info tools for plan selection and do NOT independently search for or select a different plan. Confirm to me what story that selected plan is, and provide me with an overview of the story including what is still remaining to be done without making file changes at this time.

Please check the last commit AND re-read the plan you are working on, even if you have read it before. You MUST double-check to see if tasks have been added to it by another agent or if anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the plan and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. You MUST read the whole plan again even if you have read it before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of what the plan entails.

Do you have any new requirement/scope based questions about the plan that need clarification before the plan is tasked up or worked on? Even if you have previously provided questions, maybe you have thought of some more after re-reading the plan and asking yourself `Am I sure about the full plan scope and requirements, and is it all documented within the plan`? If a '## Questions' section doesn't already exist within the plan, then create one at the end of the file, even if you do not have any questions to add. If you have questions not already in the section, then add them to the '## Questions' section as a numbered list. If the section already contains questions, then please renumber them so that the full list is ordered numerically and starts from 1. If you do not have any new questions you MUST write the exact line '- No Further Questions' immediately below the '## Questions' title, and above any existing questions within the section.

steps.label: Check for No Further Questions
steps.question: Did you add additional/new questions to the plan file (adding 'No Further Questions' does NOT count as adding questions)? You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

steps.label: Check for No Further Questions
steps.question: Re-open the plan that you are working on, ensuring that you re-read it from disk since another agent will have edited it, so what you think the plan contains is definitely out of date. Does the plan contain the line 'No Further Questions' within the '## Questions' section of the plan file? You MUST re-read the plan before giving your answer as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

content: Please read through each question listed within the '## Questions' section of the plan. For each question, I want you to perform thorough research for each question seperately in order. While researching an answer, I want you to follow these golden rules: 1. Use the KISS principle, only planning what is needed for THIS story and avoiding extra work and over engineering. Follow established patterns of what has been done before. 3. Ensure we are not going outside the scope of the oringinal plan. 4. Ensure everything we are planning to do has a purpose - To answer each question you MUST follow these steps: 1. Use the code_info tool to find out how we have approached similar issues within this repository before.  2. Use the code_info tool to find out how these kinds of issues have been approached within ALL ingested repositories. 3. Use the deepwiki and context7 tools along with web searches to confirm any questions related to external libraries that are not accessible from the code_info tool.  4. Search the web to see if other people have asked the same question, and use the best and proven solutions that match with our KISS principles.  - Once you have an answer to a question, add it to a '## Decisions' section immediately after the '## Questions' section (you will need to create it if it doesn't already exist). Each decision should be added as an item within a numbered list under the '## Decisions' section. The decision should have bullet points that indicate: - the question being addressed, WHY the question matters, WHAT the answer is, WHERE the answer came from and WHY it is the best answer to the question. In addition to adding the answer to the '## Decisions' section, you MUST update all other sections of the plan where appropriate based on the information from the answer, including description, acceptance, out of scope, etc... You MUST ALSO remove answered questions from the '## Questions' section, but never actually remove the '## Questions' section heading (in case more questions are added later). If it is impossible for you to answer a question after following the above steps, just leave it within the '## Questions' section. Never task up and commit after making any changes.
Commit all local changes, and if possible, git push the branch.
